content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Ilya and Queries". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/313/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a string s. The second line contains an integer q. The next q lines contain two integers l and r.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print the number of distinct letters in the substring from index l to r (inclusive).

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a sliding window approach or a prefix sum array to efficiently calculate the number of distinct letters in each substring.
solution.timeComplexity: O(q * length of s) in the worst case, but can be optimized to O(q + length of s) with a prefix sum array or a hash set.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(length of s) for storing the prefix sum array or hash set.

code.Python3:
s = input()
q = int(input())
for _ in range(q):
    l, r = map(int, input().split())
    count = 0
    freq = [False] * 26
    for i in range(l - 1, r):
        if not freq[ord(s[i]) - ord('a')]:
            count += 1
            freq[ord(s[i]) - ord('a')] = True
    print(count)
